task_completion.description: Verify the agent successfully logged into OpenMRS.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenMRS home dashboard or patient search screen is visible in the web browser, indicating successful authentication.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent accessed the correct patient record.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The patient chart or dashboard specifically for 'Paul Tremblay' (DOB: 1977-07-03) is clearly visible.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent accessed or initiated an active visit for the patient.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The screen shows an 'Active Visit', 'Current Visit', or visit dashboard context for Paul Tremblay, indicating the actions are taking place during a visit.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent navigated to the order entry or lab orders section.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An 'Order Entry', 'Test Order', or 'Lab Orders' interface is open within the patient's chart.

task_completion.description: Verify the Complete Blood Count (CBC) order was successfully placed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A submitted, signed, or active lab order for 'Complete Blood Count', 'CBC', or 'Blood Count' is visible in the patient's active orders list or visit summary.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the OpenMRS web interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: A web browser displaying the OpenMRS SPA is visible, rather than a terminal window executing API commands or scripts.

integrity.description: Verify the agent genuinely navigated the application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show intermediate UI states (e.g., search bars filled, dropdowns, order forms being completed) rather than just a text editor or a single final state.

integrity.description: Verify the results are from actual system state and not HTML manipulation.
integrity.visual_evidence: The UI appears consistent with standard OpenMRS styling, without browser developer tools open or awkwardly injected text.
